question: The trivalent influenza vaccine contains all of the following strains except: The options are: (A) H IN I (B) H3N2 (C) H2N 1 (D) Influenza B

Which of the following best describes the working time of impression material? The options are: (A) After the start of appearing elastic properties of impression material (B) Just after the start of appearing elastic properties of impression material (C) The time from start of mixing till just before the start of appearing elastic properties of impression material (D) Loss of lusture of impression material
Which of the following is not involved in blood clotting? The options are: (A) Calcium (B) Prothrombin (C) Fibrinogen (D) Fe+2

Which of these is not true about randomization in a clinical trial? The options are: (A) Reduces confounding (B) Decreases selection bias (C) Ensures comparability of two groups (D) Increases external validity of the trial

The least effect on the prognosis of a periodontal disease is The options are: (A) Suppuration from the pocket (B) Furcation involvement (C) Alveolar bone loss (D) Co-operation and motivation of the patient
Which of the following is supplied by the glossopharyngeal nerve? The options are: (A) Stylopharyngeus (B) Palatopharyngeus (C) Geniohyoid (D) Genioglossus
what does not occur in 5'- 3'direction? The options are: (A) DNA repair (B) DNA replication (C) RNA editing (D) Transcription

A tooth can be made to appear shorter by The options are: (A) Positioning gingival height of contour gingivally (B) Positioning gingival height of contour incisally (C) Positioning mesial and distal line angles closer (D) Positioning developmental depressions further apart
Which of the following is a method of achieving slow separation? The options are: (A) Seperating rubber bands (B) Rubber Dam Sheet (C) Ligature wire (D) All of the following
The iron carbide in orthodontic wire is in the form of: The options are: (A) Martensite (B) Ferrite (C) Carbide (D) Austenite
Juvenile periodontitis is a The options are: (A) Definite genetic disease (B) Immunological defect (C) Neutrophil chemotactic defect (D) Neutrophil adhesion defect
Which of the following is expected when a tooth is lost? The options are: (A) Decrease in trabecular pattern (B) Loss of bone width (C) Loss of bone height (D) All of the above
Cells most commonly affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy? The options are: (A) Amacrine cells (B) Bipolar cells (C) Ganglion cells (D) Rods and cones

Dimensional stability of elastomeric impression material can be given in descending order as : The options are: (A) Polysulphide > Polyether > Condensation silicone > Addition silicone (B) Polyether > Condensation silicone > Polysulphide > Addition silicone (C) Addition silicone > Polyether > Polysulphide > Condensation silicone (D) Addition silicone > Condensation silicone Polysulphide > Polyether
Radiation causes cell death by: The options are: (A) Charring of nucleoproteins (B) Destroying their mitochondria (C) Ionization (D) Disruption of cytosol
Coxsackie virus causes The options are: (A) infectious mononucleosis (B) Lymphoma (C) herpangina (D) herpes
The community periodontal index of treatment needs is recorded for: The options are: (A) Quadrants (B) Sextants (C) One arch (D) Whole dentition

In a survey of sleep apnea scores among 10 people, the highest sample of 58 was entered by mistake as 85. This will affect the result as: The options are: (A) Increased mean, decreased median (B) Increased mean, increased median (C) Increased mean. no change in median (D) No change in mean, increased median.
Crude Bih rate for a sub-center is 20. What the number of pregnant woman registered in sub-center? The options are: (A) 80 (B) 100 (C) 110 (D) 60
Surgery on the hard palate of a 3 years old cleft patient may inhibit growth, causing the facial profile to become: The options are: (A) Straight (B) Elongated (C) Shortened (D) More concave
Drugs causing hyperglycemia: The options are: (A) β-blocker (B) Glucocorticoids (C) Acetylcholine (D) α-blockers
In mechanical trituration of amalgam, what is adversely affected ? The options are: (A) Hardness of filling (B) Tarnish resistance (C) Working time (D) Final gloss of the filling
Most commonly missing tooth is The options are: (A) Upper 3rd molar (B) Lower 3rd molar (C) Upper lateral incisor (D) Lower 2nd premolar

Tensor veli palatini origin and insertion are The options are: (A) Origin Greater wing of sphenoid and scaphoid fossa; Insertion as palatine aponeurosis into posterior hard palate. (B) Origin Lesser wing of sphenoid and scaphoid fossa;Insertion Hamular notch and opposite muscle of same name (C) Origin Lesser wing of sphenoid and scaphoid fossa; Insertion- Posterior hard palate (D) Origin Lesser wing of sphenoid and scaphoid fossa; Insertion: as palatine aponeurosis into posterior hard palate.

A 10 years old child has intrusion of permanent maxillary central incisor. The choice of treatment is The options are: (A) Put tooth in its place and splint it (B) Allow tooth to erupt on its own (spontaneous eruption) (C) Treat it orthodontically (D) Do nothing

Which of the following statements regarding Kawasaki disease is true – The options are: (A) Associated with coronary artery aneurysm in up to 25% of untreated cases (B) It is the most common cause of vasculitis in children (C) IV immunoglobulins are recommended only if coronary artery is involved (D) Lymph node biopsy is used for diagnosis
The most common gene defect in idiopathic steroid resistant nephrotic syndrome – The options are: (A) ACE (B) NPHS 2 (C) HOX 11 (D) PAX
In which of the following microvilli are not present? The options are: (A) Gallbladder (B) Duodenum (C) Collecting duct (D) Proximal convoluted tubule
Ligamentum flavum consists of which fibres:- The options are: (A) Type-I collagen (B) Type-II collagen (C) Reticular (D) Elastic
About carey coombs murmur which is false – The options are: (A) Delayed diastole murmur (B) Seen in rheumatic fever (C) Can be associated with AR (D) Low pitched murmur
Pickling is done: The options are: (A) To remove oxide film from casting (B) Polish the casting (C) Improve the strength of casting (D) Avoid casting defects
Artery palpable at the anterior border of the masseter is : The options are: (A) Superficial temporal artery (B) Facial artery (C) Lingual artery (D) Maxillary artery

The following system of depiction of 32 teeth is in accordance with which system?Permanent TeethUpper RightUpper Left1234567891011121314151632313029282726252423222120191817Lower RightLower Left The options are: (A) Universal system (B) Palmer's system (C) Haderup system (D) Diagrammatic depiction

A neonate presented with jaundice on first day of life. His mother's blood group is `0' positive. How will you manage this patient? The options are: (A) Observe only as it is mostly physiological jaundice (B) Exchange transfusion (C) Liver function tests and liver biopsy as it is mostly due to cholestasis (D) Phototherapy

A blue new born presents with cyanosis. The X–ray chest reveal oligaemic lung field and normal sized heart. Most likely diagnosis is – The options are: (A) Ebstein's anomaly (B) Pulmonary atresia (C) Transposition of great arteries (D) Tetralogy of fallot

Tumor cells in chronic lymphocytic leukemia or small lymphoblastic lymphoma (CLL/SLL) arisefrom which of the following? The options are: (A) Mature B cell (B) Naive B cell (C) Centrocytes of germinal center (D) Progenitor B-cell

Difference between physical characteristic of reamers and files is: The options are: (A) The cross-section of reamers in square and files are triangular in cross-section (B) The number of flutes on the blade are more in files than in reamers (C) The reamers have more flutes in the blade (D) Files have two superficial grooves to produce flutes in a double helix design

In a 19 years old patient, with a swelling over the left angle of the mandible, temperature of 38°C and negative history of trauma, one should suspect: The options are: (A) Spontaneous fracture of the mandible (B) Pericoronal infection (C) Mumps (D) Sjogren's syndrome
Increased collum angle is seen in The options are: (A) Class I bimaxillary protrusion (B) Class II div 1 (C) Class II div 2 (D) Class III
Which of the following is an essential fatty acid? The options are: (A) Linoleic acid (B) Alpha linolenic acid (C) Both of the above (D) None of the above

A patient requires 180 mg ceftriaxone. The l contains 500 mg/5ml of ceftriaxone. You have a 2 ml syringe with l0 divisions per ml. How many divisions in the 2 ml syringe will you fill to give 180 mg ceftriaxone? The options are: (A) 9 (B) 18 (C) l0 (D) 12
For root fracture it is imperative to take X-ray at? The options are: (A) 45°,	90° (B) 90° (C) 90°,	110° (D) 45°,	90°, 110°

During cobalt chromium crown reconstruction, temperature of soldering torch suddenly increases. In such case, which of the following property will change? The options are: (A) Yield strength decreases and percent elongation increases (B) Yield strength and percent elongation both increases (C) Yield strength increases and percent elongation decreases (D) Yield strength and percent elongation both decreases

All of the following are true for ZOE based sealer except? The options are: (A) Expands on setting & hence provide impervious seal (B) Absorb if extruded periapically (C) Antibacterial, so prevent entombing of Bacterial (D) Slow setting, so results in increased inflammation initially

During laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair a tacker was accidently placed below and lateral to the ilio-pubic tract. Postoperatively the patient complained of pain and soreness in the thigh. This is due to the involvement of: The options are: (A) Lateral cutaneous nerve of thigh (B) llioinguinal nerve (C) Genital branch of genitofemoral nerve (D) Obturator nerve

A lateral sliding flap done on a tooth with roots denuded for 6 years will show The options are: (A) PDL fibres attached to cementum (B) PDL fibres attached to dentin (C) Epithelium adjacent to previously denuded root surface (D) Co adaptation to the remaining fibres
Radiation affects all three germ layers (ectoderm mesoderm and endoderm) in which of the following The options are: (A) Teratoid cyst (B) Dermoid cyst (C) Thyroglossal cyst (D) Branchial cyst
In periodontal flap most important factor to be considered is: The options are: (A) Depth of vestibule (B) Frenum attachment (C) Amount of attached gingiva (D) Free gingiva

A 70 years old hypeensive patient with stage 5 chronic kidney disease was diagnosed recently with Type 2 diabetes mellitus. He doesn't want to take injectable insulin. Which of the following oral hypoglycemic agents will be preferred in this patient, which won't require any renal dose modification? The options are: (A) Linagliptin (B) Repaglinide (C) Vildagliptin (D) Glimepiride
The odontoblasts killed during cavity preparation are derived from: The options are: (A) Unaffected odontoblasts from the pulp (B) Undifferentiated cells (C) Histocytes (D) Osteoblasts

In a 5-year-old child with mandibular fracture, on a follow up recall after 2 months, what should be done to check the bone healing: The options are: (A) OPG at every 2 month till one year (B) NCCT (C) Single contrast CT (D) Clinical examination adequate

A case of carcinoma larynx with the involvement of anterior commissure and right vocal cord, developed perichondritis of thyroid cartilage. Which of the following statements is true for the management of this case - The options are: (A) He should be given radical radiotherapy as this can cure early tumors (B) He should be treated with combination of chemotherapy and radiotherapy (C) He should first receive radiotherapy and if residual tumor is present then should undergo laryngectomy (D) He should first undergo laryngectomy and then post-operative radiotherapy
Staging of GTN with lung metastasis. The options are: (A) Stage 1 (B) Stage 2 (C) Stage 3 (D) Stage 4

Which combination forms day care anesthesia The options are: (A) Fentanyl, propofol, Isoflurane (B) Pethidine, Propofol, Isoflurane (C) Thiopental pethidime, Halothane (D) Thiopentane, isoflurane fantanyt
Sorting a patient of trauma of head injury on basis of need of care and availability of resources The options are: (A) Triage (B) Emergency care (C) Definitive care (D) Hospital care
Auriculotemporal nerve encircles which artery The options are: (A) Superficial temporal artery (B) Superficial temporal vein (C) Middle meningeal artery (D) Deep temporal

When a person lies down : The options are: (A) There is immediate increase in venous return (B) Heart rate becomes stable at slightly more than normal (C) Cerebral blood flow increases & becomes stable at more than normal (D) Blood flow to the Apex of lung decreases
Mandibular Molar is The options are: (A) Tritubercular teeth (B) Quadritubercular teeth (C) Haplodont (D) Triconodont

In dislocation of the jaw, displacement of the
articular disc beyond the articular tubercle of the temporomandibular joint results from spasm or excessive contraction of the following muscle? The options are: (A) Buccinator (B) Lateral pterygoid (C) Masseter (D) Temporalis
According to WHO, anemia in pregnancy is diagnosed, when hemoglobin is less than: The options are: (A) 10.0 gm% (B) 11.0 gm% (C) 12.0 gm% (D) 9.0 gm%

A research was undeaken by a group of psychiatrists and obstetricians to assess postpaum depression in mothers giving bih to male versus female children according to the Edinburgh depression scale (EPDS). What test should be used to compare the outcomes? The options are: (A) Student's t-test (B) Paired t-test (C) Chi-square test (D) Pearson's correlation coefficient
Infarction of anterior inferior cerebellar artery may cause damage to The options are: (A) Pyramidal tract (B) Lateral spinothalamic tract (C) Vestibular nuclei (D) Spinal nucleus of trigeminal nerve
Best method to neasure HbA1c? The options are: (A) lsoelectric focusing (B) Affinity chromatography (C) Ion exchange chromatography (D) Electrophoresis
The main vessel involved in bleeding from Juvenile nasopharyngeal angiofibroma: The options are: (A) Facial aery (B) Ascending pharyngeal aery (C) Internal maxillary aery (D) Anterior ethmoidal aery
At what stage of embryonic development does initial tooth development begins: The options are: (A) 3rd week (B) 6th week (C) 8th week (D) 4th week

A 16-year-old female with normal pubic hair and breast development, presented with complaints of primary amenorrhea. Investigation shows normal karyotype. Most likely diagnosis is:AFMC 12; JIPMER 12; AIIMS 12, 13 The options are: (A) Mullerian agenesis (B) Turner syndrome (C) Testicular feminization (D) Kallmann syndrome

A 40 year male complaining of hot flushes each time he baths. Hb: 20%gm, Platelet: 89,000/mL, WBC: 30,000/mL, Investigation revealed JAK2 mutation. What is the most likely diagnosis? The options are: (A) PMF (B) CML (C) PCV (D) Essential thrombocytosis

An anterior bite plate should be trimmed so that The options are: (A) It is included at 10° to occlusal plane (B) It is inclined at 30° to occlusal plane (C) Posterior teeth are 5 mm apart (D) Posterior teeth are 2 mm apart
Hepatitis B occurs after an incubation period of: The options are: (A) 1 week (B) 3 weeks (C) 3 months (D) 6 months

The treatment of choice for primary grade V vesicoureteric reflux involving both kidneys in a 6 month old boy is – The options are: (A) Antibiotic prophylaxis (B) Ureteric reimplantation (C) Cystoscopy followed by subureteric injection of teflon (D) Bilateral ureterostomies.

All of the following are disadvantages of composite restoration except: The options are: (A) Greater localized occlusal wear (B) Recurrent secondary caries (C) Technique sensitive (D) More time in replacement
Posselt's diagram indicates? The options are: (A) Motion of mandible (B) Protrusive relation of central incisor (C) Antero-superior relation of condyle in articular fossa in CR (D) None of the above

About Juvenile Myoclonic epilepsy, all are true EXCEPT: The options are: (A) Valproate is contraindicated (B) Lamotrigine can be given (C) Phenytoin is not the preferred drug (D) Polygenic inheritance

Acetazolamide is given to a patient of angle closure glaucoma. It is a non- competitive inhibitor of carbonic anhydrase enzyme. Which of the following should be the effect of this drug? The options are: (A) Decrease in Vmax (B) Decrease in Km (C) Decrease in both Km and Vmax (D) No change in Vmax
If 2 Implants of size 4 mm are to be placed in a ridge, what should be the minimum width required in the ridge The options are: (A) 14 mm (B) 15 mm (C) 17 mm (D) 18 mm

An adolescent school girl complaints of dropping objects from hands, it gets precipitated during morning and during exams. There is no history of loss of consciousness and her cousin sister has been diagnosed with epilepsy. EEG was done and was suggestive of epileptic spikes. What is the diagnosis? The options are: (A) Juvenile myoclonic epilepsy (B) Atypical absence (C) Choreo - athetoid epilepsy (D) Centrotemporal spikes

Which of following will happen with a flexible major connector: The options are: (A) It will make other components more effective (B) Will cause PDL destruction of abutment teeth (C) Cause ill-fitting of RPD (D) Will cause difficulty in occlusion
Post-auricular ecchymosis in cases of fracture of the base of the skull is called: The options are: (A) Battle's sign (B) Tinel's sign (C) Trousseau's sign (D) Nikolsky's sign
The means by which one part of R.P.D opposes the action of the retainer in the function is called The options are: (A) Tripoding (B) Reciprocation (C) Retention (D) Stress breaking

Which of the following is the most consistent feature of rapidly progressing glomerulonephritis (RPGN)? The options are: (A) Crescent formation (B) Mesangial cell proliferation (C) IgAdeposition (D) Loss offoot processes
The common site for necrotizing sialometaplasia The options are: (A) cheeks (B) dorsum of tongue (C) palate (D) gingival
Dense irregular connective tissue is found in The options are: (A) Dermis (B) Lamina propria (C) Tendon (D) Ligament
A 10 year old boy presents with midline swelling arising from cerebellum the diagnosis is – The options are: (A) Astrocytoma (B) Glioblastoma multiforme (C) Ependymoma (D) Medulloblastoma
Example of Dystrophic calcification- The options are: (A) Hyperparathyroidism (B) Sarcoidosis (C) Hypervitaminosis D (D) Myositis ossificans
The carat for Au foil used for restorations is: The options are: (A) 18 (B) 20 (C) 22 (D) 24
Which of the following are characteristic feature of high copper amalgam alloy? The options are: (A) Low compressive strength (B) High marginal breakdown (C) Less marginal # (D) High creep

Following intracoronal bleaching immediate composite restoration required, what has to be done: The options are: (A) Treat with catalase (B) Wait for 7 days is mandatory (C) Treat with H2O2 for 3 minutes (D) Not possible

True about Arrangement of hydroxyapatite crystals in Enamel rods? The options are: (A) In center orientation of hydroxyapatite crystal is 0 degree (B) Orientation of hydroxyapatite crystals at periphery is 45 degree (C) Both (D) None
SI unit of X-ray The options are: (A) Rad (B) Sv (C) Gray (D) Kerma
Full mouth disinfection is done in: The options are: (A) 24 hours (B) 48 hours (C) 72 hours (D) 36 hours

Treatment resins are given for which type of edentulous 
ridges? The options are: (A) Abused ridges (B) Flabby ridges (C) Ridges with large tori on them (D) Ridges with bony resorption

Use of lithium during pregnancy increases the risk of development of which of the following malformations in the baby? The options are: (A) Facial defects (B) Cardiac defects (C) Neural tube defects (D) Urogenital defects

Permanent bending of an orthodontic wire during its clinical application will leads to work hardening of the  alloy, this might cause: The options are: (A) Increase in strength (B) Decrease in ductility (C) Development of internal stresses (D) All of the above
Best way to monitor perfusion of free flap: The options are: (A) Pulse oximeter (B) Laser doppler velocimetery (C) Prick test (D) Flourescin & dermo flourometer

The decreased phosphate levels seen in hyper parathyroidism is due to: The options are: (A) Decreased intestinal phosphate absorption (B) Increased calcium excretion (C) Decreased renal phosphate absorption (D) Increased loss of phosphate in urine

Which of the following is not true regarding trigeminal nerve? The options are: (A) Roots of trigeminal nerve are attached to Pons (B) It has three main divisions (C) Trigeminal nerve supplies the dura mater of middle cranial fossa (D) The trigeminal ganglion contributes to the sensory root only
Which of the following is least sensitive to radiation exposure The options are: (A) Bone (B) Hematopoietic tissue (C) Nerve (D) Muscle
Most common cyst associated with adjoining vital teeth? The options are: (A) Dentigerous cyst (B) Globulomaxillary cyst (C) Periapical cyst (D) Lateral periodontal cyst

40 year old female came with fullness in upper right quadrant of abdomen with diabetes type II, hyperlipidemia, biopsy shows The options are: (A) Biliary cirrhosis (B) Luminal thrombosis (C) Non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (D) Autoimmune hepatitis

What is the Ilekt step in management of a 32 years old woman with a 5 years history of primary infeil-ity with bilateral tubal block seen at cornu on hys-terosalpingogram? The options are: (A) In vitro feilization (B) Laparoscopy and hysteroscopy (C) Intracytoplasmic sperm injection (D) Tuboplasty
